entries.summary: Phase 5 implemented the complete browser-side search infrastructure: Transformers.js embedding service (bge-small-en-v1.5 with OPFS caching), EdgeVec HNSW vector index (Rust/WASM with native IDB persistence), FlexSearch keyword index (resolution-based scoring), RRF fusion (faithful Python port), cross-encoder reranker (ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 with conditional activation), and memory-aware model selection. 6/6 tasks completed. Drift APPROVED. Reviewer caught critical API invention issues in EdgeVec integration (4 attempts) and Transformers.js pipeline type issues.

metadata.plan_id: HTML5 Web Version — Browser-Side Inference + API Hybrid Architecture

top_rejection_reasons: EdgeVec API invented — all method calls were non-existent
Transformers.js pipeline type wrong (feature-extraction for cross-encoder)
load() assigned index before loading metadata — inconsistent state on failure
Missing .free()/.dispose() on WASM resources causing memory leaks
FlexSearch options invented (doc: true, optimize: true don't exist on Index)
lessons_learned: EdgeVec WASM API must be verified against actual package docs — coder invented non-existent APIs (EdgeVec.Index, .add(), .remove(), .size(), .save()/load() as instance methods)
Transformers.js pipeline type matters: feature-extraction ≠ text-classification. Cross-encoder reranker MUST use text-classification, not feature-extraction
load() ordering matters for consistent state: always load metadata BEFORE assigning the main resource to prevent half-loaded states on failure
dispose() MUST call .free() or .dispose() on WASM/ONNX resources before nulling JS references — ~130MB model leaks otherwise
FlexSearch Index mode uses resolution-based scoring, NOT BM25 — comments must match actual behavior
